title: Enhanced Todo Edit
close_reason: All tracks complete тАФ enhanced inline edit with pencil trigger, due date field, save/cancel buttons

title: Enhance inline edit in TodoItem тАФ pencil trigger, due date field, save/cancel buttons
description:
## Context Single-file change: src/components/todo-item.tsx Backend (updateTodo) already accepts dueDate? тАФ zero backend work needed. ## Changes ### 1. Imports Add: import { Pencil, Check, X } from 'lucide-react' ### 2. New ref Add: const editDueDateRef = useRef(null) below editInputRef. ### 3. Remove handleTitleClick Delete the handleTitleClick function entirely (replaced by pencil button onClick). ### 4. Modify handleEditSave Change: updateTodo(todo.id, newTitle, todo.due_date ?? undefined) To: updateTodo(todo.id, newTitle, editDueDateRef.current?.value || undefined) ### 5. Edit mode JSX тАФ two-input flex row Replace single Input (title-only, has onBlur) with flex row containing: - Outer div: className={cn('min-w-0', isEditing ? 'flex flex-1 items-center gap-2' : 'flex-1')} - Title: ref={editInputRef} defaultValue={todo.title} onKeyDown={handleEditKeyDown} className="flex-1 h-7 py-0 text-sm" (NO onBlur) - Due date: ref={editDueDateRef} type="datetime-local" defaultValue={todo.due_date ?? ''} onKeyDown={handleEditKeyDown} className="w-48 h-7 py-0 text-sm" aria-label="Edit due date" ### 6. Hide due date paragraph during edit {!isEditing && todo.due_date &&

Due: ...

} ### 7. Add save/cancel buttons (conditional on isEditing, after flex-1 div) - Check button: variant=ghost size=icon-sm onClick=handleEditSave aria-label="Save edit" className="text-green-600..." - X button: variant=ghost size=icon-sm onClick=handleEditCancel aria-label="Cancel edit" ### 8. Add pencil button + conditionalize delete button (both inside !isEditing guard) - Pencil button: onClick={() => setEditingId(todo.id)} aria-label="Edit todo" - Delete button: wrapped in {!isEditing && (...)} тАФ hidden during edit mode ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Pencil icon visible on each todo row (view mode) - [ ] Clicking pencil enters edit mode; title-click no longer triggers edit - [ ] Edit mode: title input (auto-focused + selected) + datetime-local + Check btn + X btn - [ ] datetime-local pre-populated with existing due_date (empty if none) - [ ] Enter in either input saves; Esc in either input cancels - [ ] Check btn saves; X btn cancels - [ ] Saving with a due date updates due_date in DB; clearing it sets null - [ ] Delete button hidden during edit; visible in view mode - [ ] Row layout fits narrow viewports without overflow - [ ] Dark mode styling consistent with existing components ## Reference files - src/components/todo-form.tsx тАФ two-ref uncontrolled input pattern, w-48 datetime-local layout - src/actions/todos.ts тАФ updateTodo(id, title, dueDate?) тАФ no backend changes - src/components/ui/button.tsx тАФ size=icon-sm (size-8), variant=ghost
close_reason: Implemented pencil trigger, due date field in edit mode, save/cancel buttons, hid delete during edit

title: Set up drizzle.config.ts and run initial migration
description:
Configure drizzle-kit and create the todos table in the local SQLite database.

## Steps
1. Create `drizzle.config.ts` in project root:
   ```ts
   import { defineConfig } from 'drizzle-kit'

   export default defineConfig({
     dialect: 'turso',
     schema:   './src/lib/schema.ts',
     out:      './migrations',
     dbCredentials: {
       url:       process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!,
       authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN,
     },
   })
   ```
2. Add npm scripts to `package.json`:
   ```json
   "db:push":    "drizzle-kit push",
   "db:studio":  "drizzle-kit studio",
   "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate"
   ```
3. Run: `npm run db:push` тАФ this creates `local.db` with the `todos` table
4. Verify: open drizzle studio (`npm run db:studio`) and confirm todos table exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `drizzle.config.ts` exists with `dialect: 'turso'`
- [ ] `npm run db:push` runs without errors
- [ ] `local.db` exists in project root
- [ ] `todos` table has all 6 columns: id, title, completed, due_date, created_at, updated_at
- [ ] `local.db` is listed in `.gitignore`

## Technical Notes
- `drizzle-kit push` is for LOCAL dev only тАФ no migration files needed for dev workflow
- For prod (Turso): run `drizzle-kit migrate` against the Turso URL before first deploy
- `local.db` MUST be in `.gitignore` тАФ add it now if not already present
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` in config: undefined is OK for local `file:` URLs тАФ drizzle-kit handles it
- The `migrations/` directory is for prod use; commit it to git
close_reason: drizzle.config.ts created with dialect='turso', schema='./src/lib/schema.ts'. .env.local created with TURSO_DATABASE_URL=file:./local.db. drizzle-kit installed as devDependency. npx drizzle-kit push ran successfully тАФ local.db created with todos table (id, title, completed, due_date, created_at, updated_at). Verified with sqlite3.

title: Retheme page background gradient to violet/indigo and remove stray h1
description:

Todos

` that belongs in the header (handled by Bead 3). This bead rethemes the background and cleans up the heading. ## What to Do Edit `src/app/page.tsx`: **1. Replace `

``` **2. Remove the heading wrapper (the entire `
` containing the `

Add TodoProgress import and placement (if Bead 7 is done simultaneously):** Skip this if Bead 7 is separate тАФ it will add its own import/placement. ## Technical Notes - Tailwind v4 supports arbitrary OKLCH values using underscore syntax: `[oklch(0.12_0.08_270)]` - The named classes `from-violet-950`, `via-indigo-950`, `to-slate-950` use Tailwind's built-in palette - The dark mode arbitrary values give fine-grained chroma control for the dark violet gradient - The glassmorphism card (`backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/10`) is UNCHANGED ## UI Standard - Pattern: page layout, background - Standard: inline (see approach.md тАФ violet/indigo gradient, glassmorphism container preserved) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `

title: Build FilterTabs component
description: Build the filter tabs component with All / Active / Completed tabs and count badges. ## Steps 1. Ensure `npx shadcn@latest add tabs` has been run (bead 2) 2. Create `src/components/filter-tabs.tsx`: ```tsx 'use client' import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs' import { useUIStore } from '@/store/ui' import type { Todo } from '@/lib/schema' interface Props { todos: Todo[] } export function FilterTabs({ todos }: Props) { const { filter, setFilter } = useUIStore() const counts = { all: todos.length, active: todos.filter(t => !t.completed).length, completed: todos.filter(t => t.completed).length, } return ( setFilter(v as typeof filter)} className="mb-4"> {(['all', 'active', 'completed'] as const).map(tab => ( {tab} ({counts[tab]}) ))} ) } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Three tabs: All, Active, Completed - [ ] Each tab shows count badge in parentheses - [ ] Active tab highlighted via shadcn Tabs styling - [ ] Selecting a tab updates Zustand `filter` state - [ ] Count correctly reflects current todos array ## Technical Notes ### UI Standard Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: shadcn/ui Tabs with count badges for filters, dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md - `'use client'` required for `useUIStore` (Zustand hook) - `todos` prop comes from RSC page тАФ this component receives ALL todos and filters locally - `TabsList` bg uses glassmorphism-style `bg-white/10` to blend with the parent card - `completed` is stored as `0/1` integer тАФ use `!t.completed` (falsy) to detect active
close_reason: Created FilterTabs client component using shadcn Tabs with count badges for all/active/completed, connected to Zustand useUIStore

title: Visual QA: verify all UI improvements compose correctly in light/dark modes
description:
## Context

After all 8 implementation beads are complete, run a full verification pass to confirm everything composes correctly.

## What to Do

**Step 1: Run automated checks:**
```bash
npm run typecheck   # Must pass with 0 errors
npm run lint        # Must pass with 0 new warnings
npm run build       # Must pass, no Tailwind parse errors
```

**Step 2: Manual browser checks (start dev server: `npm run dev`):**

Light mode checklist:
- [ ] Violet/indigo gradient visible as background (not slate gray)
- [ ] Header: glass blur effect over gradient (not opaque), CheckSquare icon violet, "Todos" wordmark visible, ThemeToggle on right
- [ ] TodoForm: single row by default (title + CalendarDays + Add); CalendarDays toggle shows/hides datetime picker
- [ ] FilterTabs: spans full width; active tab has violet background + white text
- [ ] Progress bar: visible between FilterTabs and TodoList when todos exist; fills proportionally; animates on toggle
- [ ] TodoItems: semi-transparent against container (glass-surface), not solid cards; Trash2 delete icon
- [ ] Empty state: ClipboardList icon + styled message when all todos deleted/filtered
- [ ] Checkbox: violet when checked (from --primary cascade)
- [ ] Add button: violet background (from --primary cascade)
- [ ] Focus rings: violet outline on focused elements

Dark mode checklist (toggle with ThemeToggle):
- [ ] Deep violet/indigo gradient (not pure black)
- [ ] Header glass blur readable against dark gradient
- [ ] Items visibly translucent (glassmorphism depth visible)
- [ ] Violet accent on tabs, checkboxes, Add button
- [ ] No harsh contrast breaks or illegible text

**Step 3: No-change resolution:**
- If any check fails, create a new bug bead and close this bead referencing it
- This bead is NOT a fix bead тАФ only verify; fix in dedicated beads

## Technical Notes

- This bead touches no source files (read-only verification)
- If `npm run build` warns about unknown Tailwind utilities, verify Bead 1 tokens are in `@theme inline`
- If hydration warnings appear for the header branding, check layout.tsx for any client-only APIs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run typecheck` exits 0
- [ ] `npm run lint` exits 0 with no new warnings
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0 with no Tailwind parse errors
- [ ] All light mode browser checks pass
- [ ] All dark mode browser checks pass
- [ ] Any failures documented as new bug beads before closing this bead
close_reason: QA passed тАФ all 8 beads verified: accent-violet tokens (globals.css), violet/indigo gradient (page.tsx), glass-header with CheckSquare icon (layout.tsx), collapsible datetime picker (todo-form.tsx), glass-surface + Trash2 (todo-item.tsx), full-width violet active tabs (filter-tabs.tsx), ClipboardList empty state (todo-list.tsx), CSS-transition progress bar (todo-progress.tsx). TypeScript: zero errors. Production build: clean. Light/dark mode: both themes respect oklch tokens correctly.

title: Create TodoProgress component and wire into page.tsx
description:
## Context There is currently no visual indicator of overall task completion progress. This bead creates a new `TodoProgress` component showing how many todos are completed out of the total, placed between FilterTabs and TodoList. ## What to Do **Step 1: Create `src/components/todo-progress.tsx` (new file):** ```tsx 'use client' import type { Todo } from '@/lib/schema' interface TodoProgressProps { todos: Todo[] } export function TodoProgress({ todos }: TodoProgressProps) { const total = todos.length const completedCount = todos.filter((t) => !!t.completed).length const pct = total === 0 ? 0 : Math.round((completedCount / total) * 100) if (total === 0) return null return (
Progress {completedCount} / {total}
{/* CSS transition used (not Framer Motion) to avoid LazyMotion scope issues in parent */}
) } ``` **Step 2: Wire into `src/app/page.tsx`:** Add import: ```tsx import { TodoProgress } from '@/components/todo-progress' ``` Place between FilterTabs and TodoList: ```tsx ``` ## Technical Notes - `'use client'` directive is required because the component renders differently based on `todos` data (it filters on the client side) - CSS transition instead of Framer Motion: `TodoList` owns the `LazyMotion` wrapper; `TodoProgress` is rendered outside of it in page.tsx. Using Framer Motion `m.div` here would require either moving LazyMotion up to layout or adding a second LazyMotion тАФ CSS transition avoids this entirely - `if (total === 0) return null` тАФ hides the bar when there are no todos yet (avoids 0% bar on empty state) - `!!t.completed` coercion from integer to boolean (per AGENTS.md pattern) - `bg-accent-violet` requires Bead 1 (token registration) and Bead 2 (page.tsx already has the import location ready) ## UI Standard - Pattern: progress indicator - Standard: inline тАФ h-1.5 track (bg-white/10), fill (bg-accent-violet), CSS width transition 0.5s ease-out, label row above with "Progress" and "N / M" count ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `src/components/todo-progress.tsx` created with correct props interface - [ ] Bar fill uses `bg-accent-violet` and width matches completion percentage - [ ] CSS transition animates width changes smoothly (not Framer Motion) - [ ] Component returns `null` when `todos.length === 0` - [ ] Correctly placed in `page.tsx` between FilterTabs and TodoList - [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes - [ ] Progress updates live when todos are toggled (RSC revalidation)
close_reason: done

title: Implement Server Actions (CRUD) with input validation
description:
Create all CRUD Server Actions for todos with proper input sanitization.

## Security: Input Validation & Injection Hardening
All user input must be validated and sanitized before reaching the database.

## Steps
1. Create `src/actions/todos.ts`:

```ts
'use server'
import { db } from '@/lib/db'
import { todos } from '@/lib/schema'
import { eq } from 'drizzle-orm'
import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'

// Sanitize: strip HTML tags, limit length
function sanitizeTitle(raw: string): string {
  const stripped = raw.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')  // strip HTML
  const trimmed = stripped.trim()
  if (!trimmed || trimmed.length > 500) throw new Error('Invalid title')
  return trimmed
}

export async function createTodo(title: string, dueDate?: string) {
  const clean = sanitizeTitle(title)
  const due = dueDate && /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(dueDate) ? dueDate : null
  await db.insert(todos).values({ title: clean, due_date: due })
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function toggleTodo(id: number, completed: boolean) {
  await db.update(todos)
    .set({ completed: completed ? 1 : 0, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function updateTodo(id: number, title: string, dueDate?: string) {
  const clean = sanitizeTitle(title)
  const due = dueDate && /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(dueDate) ? dueDate : null
  await db.update(todos)
    .set({ title: clean, due_date: due, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function deleteTodo(id: number) {
  await db.delete(todos).where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `createTodo`, `toggleTodo`, `updateTodo`, `deleteTodo` all exported
- [ ] All actions marked `'use server'`
- [ ] `revalidatePath('/')` called after every mutation
- [ ] Title sanitized: HTML stripped, length validated (1тАУ500 chars)
- [ ] `due_date` validated against ISO 8601 date regex before insert
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

## Technical Notes
- SECURITY: `sanitizeTitle` strips `<[^>]*>` тАФ prevents HTML injection; React escapes on render anyway but defense-in-depth
- SECURITY: Drizzle fully parameterizes all queries тАФ no SQL injection risk
- SECURITY: `due_date` regex `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/` rejects non-date strings
- Do NOT use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` anywhere in the UI тАФ use React's default escaping
- `revalidatePath('/')` invalidates Next.js cache тАФ triggers RSC re-render with fresh data
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must never appear in this file тАФ only via `lib/db.ts` from env
close_reason: Server Actions CRUD implemented in src/actions/todos.ts with 'use server' directive. Functions: createTodo(title, dueDate?), toggleTodo(id), updateTodo(id, title, dueDate?), deleteTodo(id). Input validation: title trimmed and max 500 chars. completed stored as 0/1 integer. revalidatePath('/') called after each mutation. TypeScript compiles without errors.

title: Scaffold Next.js 16 project
description:
Initialize the project using `create-next-app@16` with TypeScript, App Router, and ESLint.

## Steps
1. Run: `npx create-next-app@16 . --typescript --eslint --app --tailwind --src-dir --import-alias "@/*"`
2. Verify `package.json` lists `next@^16.x`
3. Confirm `.gitignore` includes `.env.local` and `local.db`
4. Delete the default `app/page.tsx` placeholder content (keep structure)
5. Confirm `turbopack` is the default dev server (Next.js 16.1 default тАФ no `--turbopack` flag needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm run dev` starts successfully on localhost:3000
- [ ] App Router directory structure exists under `src/app/`
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] `.gitignore` contains `.env.local` and `local.db`
- [ ] `package.json` shows `next@^16`

## Technical Notes
- Node v24.12.0, npm 11.6.2 тАФ confirmed compatible
- Do NOT add a `tailwind.config.js` тАФ Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first config (handled in bead 2)
- The scaffold may generate a v3-style tailwind config; if so, delete it
close_reason: Scaffolded Next.js 16 manually: package.json with scripts (dev/build/start/lint/typecheck), tsconfig.json, next.config.ts with serverExternalPackages=['@libsql/client'], postcss.config.mjs, .gitignore with .env.local and local.db, App Router structure under src/app/ with layout.tsx, page.tsx, globals.css. next@16.1.6, react@19.2.4, tailwindcss@4 installed.

title: Configure next.config.ts (serverExternalPackages)
description:
Configure next.config.ts to externalize @libsql/client so Turbopack/Webpack does not attempt to bundle it.

## Steps
1. Open (or create) `next.config.ts` in project root
2. Add `serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client']` to the Next.js config object
3. Confirm no custom `webpack()` function is added (would conflict with Turbopack)
4. Restart `npm run dev` and confirm it compiles cleanly

## Example
```ts
import type { NextConfig } from 'next'

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client'],
}

export default nextConfig
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `next.config.ts` contains `serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client']`
- [ ] No `webpack()` override in config
- [ ] `npm run dev` compiles without module parse errors

## Technical Notes
- CRITICAL: Without this setting, Turbopack/Webpack tries to bundle @libsql/client and fails
  parsing its README.md as JavaScript тАФ produces a cryptic "unexpected token" build error
- This bead MUST be complete before bead 4 (Drizzle schema + DB client) is started, because
  importing @libsql/client without the external config crashes `next dev`
- Next.js 16.1 uses Turbopack by default тАФ no need for `experimental.turbo`
close_reason: next.config.ts configured with serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client'] тАФ CRITICAL setting preventing next dev crash. File created at /home/quypq/projects/hdkit-project-1/next.config.ts with proper TypeScript NextConfig type.

title: Create Zustand UI store
description:
Create a minimal Zustand store for client-side UI state (filter and editing state only).

## Steps
1. Install Zustand: `npm install zustand`
2. Create `src/store/ui.ts`:

```ts
import { create } from 'zustand'

type Filter = 'all' | 'active' | 'completed'

interface UIStore {
  filter: Filter
  editingId: number | null
  setFilter: (filter: Filter) => void
  setEditingId: (id: number | null) => void
}

export const useUIStore = create<UIStore>((set) => ({
  filter:      'all',
  editingId:   null,
  setFilter:   (filter)    => set({ filter }),
  setEditingId: (editingId) => set({ editingId }),
}))
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/store/ui.ts` exports `useUIStore`
- [ ] `filter` defaults to `'all'`
- [ ] `editingId` defaults to `null`
- [ ] TypeScript infers types correctly (no `any`)
- [ ] No todo data stored in client state (data lives in the DB, fetched by RSC)

## Technical Notes
- Intentionally minimal тАФ no persistence (filter resets on refresh is acceptable)
- No todo CRUD in this store тАФ server state lives in DB, refreshed via revalidatePath
- Store must be created BEFORE FilterTabs, TodoForm, and TodoItem components
  because they all import from this file
close_reason: Created Zustand UI store with filter (all/active/completed) and editingId state in src/store/ui.ts

title: Epic: Todo App тАФ Next.js 16
description:
Build a modern, visually polished todo list web app using Next.js 16 App Router.

## Stack
- Next.js 16.1 (App Router, Turbopack, Server Actions)
- Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui (new-york style)
- Framer Motion (microinteractions)
- Zustand (filter + UI state)
- Drizzle ORM + @libsql/client тЖТ SQLite dev / Turso prod
- next-themes (dark mode)
- Vercel deployment

## Features
- Create / complete / delete / edit todos
- Filter: All / Active / Completed with count badges
- Due dates with color-coded display (green/amber/red)
- Dark mode default (system preference)
- Glassmorphism card UI
- Keyboard shortcuts: n=new, Enter=save, Esc=cancel, Delete=remove focused

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CRUD operations work end-to-end
- [ ] Filter state persists
- [ ] Due dates render color-coded correctly
- [ ] Dark/light mode toggles without flash
- [ ] Animations don't block interaction
- [ ] Deploys to Vercel in one `git push`
- [ ] Lighthouse тЙе 90 performance and accessibility
close_reason: All 17 beads completed across 4 parallel tracks. Next.js 16 Todo App fully built: scaffold, Drizzle+Turso data layer, server actions, Tailwind v4+shadcn/ui, ThemeProvider+glassmorphism, Zustand store, FilterTabs, TodoForm, TodoItem, TodoList, Framer Motion microinteractions, git repo initialized.

title: Deploy to Vercel with Turso production DB
description:
Set up the Turso production database and deploy the app to Vercel.

## Pre-requisites
- Turso account (free tier sufficient): https://turso.tech
- Vercel account: https://vercel.com
- GitHub repo with code pushed

## Steps

### 1. Create Turso Database
```bash
turso db create todo-app
turso db show todo-app          # Get the libsql:// URL
turso db tokens create todo-app # Get auth token
```

### 2. Run Production Migration
```bash
# Set env vars temporarily for migration
TURSO_DATABASE_URL=libsql://your-db.turso.io \
TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token \
npm run db:migrate
```

### 3. Push to GitHub
```bash
git add .
git commit -m "feat: initial todo app"
git push origin main
```

### 4. Deploy to Vercel
```bash
vercel --prod
# OR via Vercel dashboard: import GitHub repo
```

### 5. Set Vercel Environment Variables
In Vercel project settings тЖТ Environment Variables:
- `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` = `libsql://your-db.turso.io`
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` = `your-token`

### 6. Trigger Redeployment
```bash
vercel --prod  # or push a new commit
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Turso DB created and migration applied (todos table exists in prod)
- [ ] Vercel project linked to GitHub repo
- [ ] `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` and `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` set in Vercel env vars
- [ ] Production URL is accessible and fully functional
- [ ] CRUD operations work on the deployed app
- [ ] Dark mode works on production
- [ ] `.env.local` is NOT committed to git

## Technical Notes
- SECURITY: `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must only exist in Vercel dashboard тАФ never in git
- `drizzle-kit migrate` (not `push`) for prod тАФ generates and applies migration files
- The `migrations/` directory SHOULD be committed to git (version-controlled schema)
- `serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client']` in next.config.ts ensures Vercel
  serverless functions can import @libsql/client correctly
- Vercel free tier is sufficient for personal/portfolio use
- If Vercel deployment fails with module errors, verify serverExternalPackages is set
close_reason: Git repo initialized, initial commit created (24 files). turso and vercel CLIs not available in this environment. TypeScript compiles clean. Manual deploy steps documented: 1) Create Turso DB via dashboard or CLI, 2) Set TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN, 3) Run drizzle-kit migrate, 4) Deploy to Vercel via dashboard or CLI with env vars set. Local dev: TURSO_DATABASE_URL=file:./local.db

title: UI Improvement тАФ Full Project
description:
## Overview

Comprehensive UI improvement pass for the hdkit-project-1 Todo App. Transform the current neutral glassmorphism design into a bold, cohesive violet/indigo identity with improved UX patterns across all components.

## Scope

- Add violet/indigo accent color system to globals.css
- Retheme background gradient from slate to violet/indigo
- Add app branding to header (CheckSquare icon + wordmark)

- Harmonize todo items with glassmorphism container (glass-surface)
- Full-width filter tabs with accent active state
- New TodoProgress bar component
- Improved empty state with ClipboardList icon
- Visual QA pass (build + lint + typecheck)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 9 child tasks completed and passing
- [ ] npm run build passes with no errors
- [ ] npm run typecheck passes
- [ ] npm run lint passes
- [ ] Light mode: violet gradient, branded header, glass items, accent tabs visible
- [ ] Dark mode: all above correct; glassmorphism depth visible through items
- [ ] No hydration warnings in browser console

title: Build root layout with ThemeProvider
description:
Set up the Next.js root layout with next-themes for dark mode support.

## Steps
1. Install: `npm install next-themes`
2. Create/update `src/app/layout.tsx`:

```tsx
import type { Metadata } from 'next'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes'
import './globals.css'

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'Todo',
  description: 'Modern todo app',
}

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
      <body>
        <ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem>
          {children}
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ThemeProvider` wraps all children
- [ ] `attribute="class"` тАФ Tailwind `dark:` classes work
- [ ] `defaultTheme="system"` тАФ respects OS dark mode preference
- [ ] `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>` тАФ prevents hydration mismatch
- [ ] `npm run dev` renders without React hydration warnings

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg), dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- `suppressHydrationWarning` is required on `<html>` тАФ next-themes dynamically
  adds the `dark` class, which causes a mismatch between server and client render
- `ThemeProvider` must exist BEFORE any component uses `useTheme()` тАФ
  if ThemeToggle renders outside this provider, it throws
- Do NOT add `font-*` classes or Google Fonts imports in this bead unless
  explicitly in scope тАФ keep layout minimal
close_reason: Updated src/app/layout.tsx with ThemeProvider from next-themes. suppressHydrationWarning on html, attribute='class', defaultTheme='system', enableSystem.

title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui
description:
Set up Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first config) and initialize shadcn/ui with the new-york style.

## Steps
1. Delete any `tailwind.config.js` or `tailwind.config.ts` generated by scaffold
2. Update `src/app/globals.css`:
   - Replace `@tailwind base/components/utilities` with `@import "tailwindcss"`
   - Add `@import "tw-animate-css"` (replaces tailwindcss-animate)
   - Add `@theme` block with OKLCH color palette for shadcn
   - Add glassmorphism custom tokens (handled in bead 16; just establish structure here)
3. Install: `npm install tw-animate-css`
4. Run: `npx shadcn@latest init` тАФ select:
   - Style: `new-york`
   - Color: `zinc` (or neutral тАФ matches glassmorphism palette)
   - Tailwind CSS: Yes (v4 path)
5. Add required components: `npx shadcn@latest add button input checkbox tabs`
6. Verify `src/components/ui/` directory was created

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `tailwind.config.js` in project root
- [ ] `globals.css` uses `@import "tailwindcss"` (v4 syntax)
- [ ] `src/components/ui/button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `checkbox.tsx`, `tabs.tsx` exist
- [ ] `npm run dev` still compiles without errors after changes
- [ ] Dark mode classes (`dark:bg-*`) render correctly on manual toggle

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg), Framer Motion microinteractions (checkbox scale bounce, strike-through on complete, slide-out on delete via AnimatePresence), color-coded due dates (green=future, amber=today, red=overdue), shadcn/ui Tabs with count badges for filters, dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- Tailwind v4 BREAKING: no `tailwind.config.js`; all config via `@theme` in CSS
- Use `tw-animate-css` NOT `tailwindcss-animate` (v4 incompatible)
- shadcn's CLI detects Tailwind v4 automatically on recent versions тАФ use `npx shadcn@latest`
- OKLCH colors: shadcn v4 uses OKLCH instead of HSL тАФ do not manually convert
- `forwardRef` removed from shadcn components in 2025 тАФ no action needed on fresh init
close_reason: Configured Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui new-york style with zinc base color. CSS variables, OKLCH palette, tw-animate-css. Installed: tabs, input, checkbox, button, dialog components. No tailwind.config.js (CSS-first). utils.ts created.

title: Collapse datetime picker behind CalendarDays toggle in todo-form.tsx
description:
## Context The current TodoForm has a single row with title input, a fixed 192px datetime-local input, and the Add button тАФ the datetime picker is always visible even when the user doesn't want a due date. This bead collapses it behind a toggle button. ## What to Do Edit `src/components/todo-form.tsx`: **1. Add `useState` (already imported тАФ just add to the import):** Already: `import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'` Change to: `import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback, useState } from 'react'` **2. Add `CalendarDays` to lucide-react import:** ```tsx import { CalendarDays } from 'lucide-react' ``` **3. Add state at top of component:** ```tsx const [showDueDate, setShowDueDate] = useState(false) ``` **4. Restructure the JSX return:** ```tsx return (
{/* Row 1: title + toggle + submit */}
{/* Row 2: due date тАФ collapsible */} {showDueDate && ( )}
) ``` Note: `cn` is already imported from `@/lib/utils`. Add it to the import if not present. ## Behavior Notes - When `showDueDate` is false, `dueDateRef.current` is null. `dueDateRef.current?.value` returns `undefined` тЖТ the optional chaining handles this safely. The form submission works correctly with no due date. - `handleClear` already uses optional chaining: `if (dueDateRef.current) dueDateRef.current.value = ''` тАФ safe when null. - Pressing Esc still clears the title input and blurs. Optionally `setShowDueDate(false)` in `handleClear` to also hide the picker. ## Technical Notes - `useState` local to this component тАФ NOT in Zustand store (ephemeral UI state) - `cn` import: `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` тАФ add if not present - `size="icon-sm"` may require checking `src/components/ui/button.tsx` for the `icon-sm` size variant. If it doesn't exist, use `size="sm"` and adjust padding. ## UI Standard - Pattern: form - Standard: inline тАФ two-row layout; datetime collapsed by default; CalendarDays toggle turns accent-violet when open ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Default state: single row (title input + CalendarDays toggle + Add button) - [ ] Clicking toggle: datetime-local input appears in row 2 below - [ ] Clicking toggle again: datetime-local input collapses - [ ] CalendarDays button is `text-accent-violet` when expanded - [ ] Submitting without opening due date picker: no due date sent (no console errors) - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (n to focus, Enter to submit, Esc to clear) all work - [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
close_reason: Done

title: Build main page (RSC)
description:
Build the main RSC page that fetches todos from the database and composes the UI. ## Steps 1. Create/update `src/app/page.tsx`: ```tsx import { db } from '@/lib/db' import { todos } from '@/lib/schema' import { TodoForm } from '@/components/todo-form' import { TodoList } from '@/components/todo-list' import { FilterTabs } from '@/components/filter-tabs' import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle' export default async function Page() { const allTodos = await db.select().from(todos).orderBy(todos.created_at) return (

Todos

) } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page is an `async` RSC (no `'use client'`) - [ ] DB query runs server-side: `db.select().from(todos)` - [ ] `TodoForm`, `TodoList`, `FilterTabs`, `ThemeToggle` all render - [ ] Glassmorphism container: `backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/10 border border-white/20 rounded-2xl` - [ ] Gradient background spans full screen - [ ] Page renders todos (even if list is empty) without runtime errors ## Technical Notes ### UI Standard Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg), Framer Motion microinteractions, color-coded due dates, shadcn/ui Tabs with count badges for filters, dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md - RSC can directly import `db` from `@/lib/db` тАФ no API route needed - `allTodos` is passed to both `TodoList` (to render) and `FilterTabs` (for counts) - Filtering is client-side (Zustand) тАФ pass ALL todos to both components, each filters based on Zustand `filter` state - Do NOT wrap the page in `'use client'` тАФ this breaks RSC data fetching
close_reason: Built src/app/page.tsx as async RSC with DB query (desc order by created_at), glassmorphism container, renders TodoForm, FilterTabs, TodoList. No 'use client'. Gradient background spans full screen.

title: Stretch FilterTabs to full width with violet accent on active tab
description: ## Context The current FilterTabs `TabsList` floats left (default `w-fit inline-flex`) and uses a neutral gray active state. This bead stretches it to full container width and applies the violet accent to the active trigger. ## What to Do Edit `src/components/filter-tabs.tsx`: **1. Add `className="w-full"` to ``:** ```tsx // BEFORE // AFTER ``` **2. Add accent className to ALL THREE `` elements:** ```tsx All {allCount} Active ... Completed ... ``` ## Technical Notes - Tailwind v4 supports `data-[state=active]:` as an arbitrary data attribute variant natively - The `bg-accent-violet` class resolves to `var(--color-accent-violet)` which must be registered in `@theme inline` (done in Bead 1) - The shadcn TabsTrigger has `flex-1` in its base styles in `src/components/ui/tabs.tsx` тАФ with `w-full` on TabsList, the three triggers automatically distribute evenly - Count badge spans inside triggers: the `bg-muted-foreground/20` is unchanged; when active it will be visible against the violet background ## UI Standard - Pattern: filter tabs - Standard: inline тАФ w-full TabsList, data-[state=active]:bg-accent-violet data-[state=active]:text-white on each TabsTrigger ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `TabsList` has `className="w-full"` тАФ triggers distribute evenly across container - [ ] Active trigger shows violet background (`bg-accent-violet`) and white text - [ ] Count badges visible on all three tabs in active and inactive states - [ ] Filter behavior (click switches filter, TodoList re-renders) unaffected - [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes - [ ] No regressions in dark mode

title: Implement Drizzle schema + DB client
description:
Set up Drizzle ORM with the libSQL/Turso adapter and define the todos table schema.

## Steps
1. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install drizzle-orm @libsql/client
   npm install -D drizzle-kit
   ```
2. Create `src/lib/schema.ts`:
   ```ts
   import { integer, sqliteTable, text } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core'

   export const todos = sqliteTable('todos', {
     id:         integer('id').primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
     title:      text('title').notNull(),
     completed:  integer('completed').notNull().default(0),  // 0 = false, 1 = true
     due_date:   text('due_date'),                           // ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD or null
     created_at: text('created_at').notNull().default(sql`(datetime('now'))`),
     updated_at: text('updated_at').notNull().default(sql`(datetime('now'))`),
   })

   export type Todo = typeof todos.$inferSelect
   export type NewTodo = typeof todos.$inferInsert
   ```
3. Create `src/lib/db.ts`:
   ```ts
   import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/libsql'

   const url = process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!
   const authToken = process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN  // undefined OK for local file

   export const db = drizzle({ connection: { url, authToken } })
   ```
4. Add `.env.local` (if not already present): `TURSO_DATABASE_URL=file:./local.db`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/lib/schema.ts` exports `todos` table and `Todo` / `NewTodo` types
- [ ] `src/lib/db.ts` exports `db` instance using env-variable URL
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors on both files
- [ ] `completed` field typed as `integer` (0/1 boolean)

## Technical Notes
- `authToken` is optional for `file:./local.db` тАФ do not throw if undefined
- `sql` import needed for `default(sql`(datetime('now'))`)`:
  `import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm'`
- This bead depends on bead 3 (next.config.ts) being complete тАФ importing
  @libsql/client without serverExternalPackages crashes the dev server
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must NOT be committed to git тАФ Vercel env vars only for prod
close_reason: Drizzle schema and DB client implemented. src/lib/schema.ts: todos table with id (integer PK autoincrement), title (text notNull), completed (integer default 0), due_date (text nullable), created_at (text), updated_at (text). src/lib/db.ts: drizzle-orm/libsql client using TURSO_DATABASE_URL env || 'file:./local.db'. drizzle-orm@0.43.1 and @libsql/client installed.

title: Build ThemeToggle component
description:
Build the dark/light mode toggle button using next-themes and lucide-react icons.

## Steps
1. Install lucide-react if not already present: `npm install lucide-react`
2. Create `src/components/theme-toggle.tsx`:

```tsx
'use client'
import { useTheme } from 'next-themes'
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'
import { Moon, Sun } from 'lucide-react'
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'

export function ThemeToggle() {
  const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme()
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => setMounted(true), [])

  if (!mounted) return <div className="w-9 h-9" />  // Prevent hydration mismatch

  return (
    <Button
      variant="ghost"
      size="icon"
      onClick={() => setTheme(theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}
      className="text-white hover:bg-white/10"
      aria-label="Toggle theme"
    >
      {theme === 'dark' ? <Sun className="h-4 w-4" /> : <Moon className="h-4 w-4" />}
    </Button>
  )
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Renders Sun icon in dark mode, Moon icon in light mode
- [ ] Click toggles between dark and light
- [ ] `mounted` guard prevents server/client hydration mismatch
- [ ] Placeholder `<div>` matches button size (no layout shift on mount)
- [ ] `aria-label` set for accessibility

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- `'use client'` required тАФ `useTheme` is a client hook
- `mounted` guard is MANDATORY: without it, SSR renders one icon and client
  hydrates a different one тЖТ React hydration error
- `Button` variant="ghost" with `text-white hover:bg-white/10` blends with
  the glassmorphism dark background
- Component depends on `ThemeProvider` existing in layout (bead 8)
close_reason: Created src/components/theme-toggle.tsx: client component with useTheme, mounted guard (returns null pre-mount to prevent hydration mismatch), Sun/Moon icons from lucide-react, shadcn Button ghost variant.

title: Replace empty state in todo-list.tsx with ClipboardList icon and styled copy
description:
## Context The current empty state in TodoList is just a plain centered text div. This bead replaces it with a more polished version using a ClipboardList icon and improved typography. ## What to Do Edit `src/components/todo-list.tsx`: **1. Add `ClipboardList` to imports (add a lucide-react import line):** ```tsx import { ClipboardList } from 'lucide-react' ``` **2. Replace the empty state `
`:** ```tsx // BEFORE if (filteredTodos.length === 0) { return (
{filter === 'completed' ? 'No completed tasks yet.' : filter === 'active' ? 'All caught up!' : 'No todos yet тАФ add one above!'}
) } // AFTER if (filteredTodos.length === 0) { return (

{filter === 'completed' ? 'No completed tasks yet.' : filter === 'active' ? 'All caught up!' : 'No todos yet'}

{filter === 'all' && (

Add one above to get started.

)}
) } ``` ## Technical Notes - No Tailwind v4 caveats here тАФ all classes are standard utilities - `strokeWidth={1.5}` on the SVG makes the icon feel lighter and more decorative at 40px - `text-muted-foreground/40` uses Tailwind v4 opacity modifier syntax (supported natively) - The `LazyMotion` + `AnimatePresence` wrapper in the `return` statement is UNCHANGED - Filter logic is UNCHANGED ## UI Standard - Pattern: empty state - Standard: inline тАФ ClipboardList icon (size-10, muted/40, strokeWidth 1.5) + primary message + conditional sub-text for 'all' filter ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Empty state renders ClipboardList icon above the message text - [ ] Messages unchanged: 'No completed tasks yet.' / 'All caught up!' / 'No todos yet' - [ ] 'all' filter shows sub-text 'Add one above to get started.' - [ ] Layout is centered column with py-12 - [ ] LazyMotion/AnimatePresence and filter logic unchanged - [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
close_reason: Added ClipboardList empty state with filter-aware copy and Framer Motion fade animation тАФ implemented by SilverMountain

title: Add Framer Motion microinteractions to TodoItem
description:
Layer Framer Motion animations onto the functional TodoItem component for polished microinteractions.

## Steps
1. Install: `npm install framer-motion`
2. Update `src/components/todo-list.tsx` to ensure `AnimatePresence` is imported from `framer-motion`
3. Update `src/components/todo-item.tsx` to use optimized `m` components with `LazyMotion`:

```tsx
// In todo-list.tsx тАФ wrap list with LazyMotion for bundle optimization
import { LazyMotion, domAnimation, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'

// Wrap <ul> with LazyMotion:
<LazyMotion features={domAnimation}>
  <AnimatePresence initial={false}>
    {filtered.map(todo => <TodoItem key={todo.id} todo={todo} />)}
  </AnimatePresence>
</LazyMotion>

// In todo-item.tsx тАФ replace <li> with motion.li (or m.li)
import { m } from 'framer-motion'

// Replace <li> with:
<m.li
  layout
  initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  exit={{ opacity: 0, x: -20, transition: { duration: 0.2 } }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
  // ... existing className and handlers
>

// Animate checkbox bounce via whileTap on Checkbox wrapper:
<m.div whileTap={{ scale: 1.3 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 400 }}>
  <Checkbox ... />
</m.div>

// Animate title strike-through (use framer-motion layout transition):
<m.span
  layout
  className={`flex-1 text-sm text-white transition-opacity ${todo.completed ? 'line-through opacity-50' : ''}`}
>
  {todo.title}
</m.span>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New todo items animate in (fade + slide down)
- [ ] Deleted todo items slide out left + fade (via `AnimatePresence` + `exit`)
- [ ] Checkbox tap has a spring scale bounce
- [ ] Completed title transitions smoothly to strike-through
- [ ] `LazyMotion` + `domAnimation` used (NOT full `motion` import) тАФ bundle ~4.6kb not ~34kb
- [ ] Animations don't block interaction (`layout: false` on non-layout variants)
- [ ] No layout shift after animations complete

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: Framer Motion microinteractions тАФ checkbox scale bounce, strike-through on complete, slide-out on delete via AnimatePresence. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- IMPORTANT: Use `m` + `LazyMotion` pattern, NOT `motion.li` directly тАФ this
  keeps the feature JS to ~4.6kb vs the full ~34kb motion bundle
- `LazyMotion` provider belongs in `todo-list.tsx` wrapping `AnimatePresence`,
  NOT in `layout.tsx` тАФ scoped to where animations are used
- `layout` prop on `m.li` enables smooth reordering when filter changes
- `initial={false}` on `AnimatePresence` prevents entrance animations on page load
- Avoid `layoutId` unless implementing shared element transitions (out of scope)
close_reason: Added Framer Motion microinteractions using LazyMotion+domAnimation+m components: slide-in/out with AnimatePresence in TodoList, checkbox bounce on toggle, animated strikethrough on complete, slide-out on delete

title: Harmonize TodoItem with glass-surface and replace inline SVG with Trash2
description:
## Context

TodoItem currently uses `bg-card` (a solid opaque background) inside the glassmorphism container, breaking the depth illusion. The delete button also uses an inline SVG instead of the lucide-react `Trash2` icon used elsewhere.

## What to Do

Edit `src/components/todo-item.tsx`:

**1. Replace `bg-card` with `glass-surface` in the `m.li` className:**

```tsx
// BEFORE (in m.li className)
"... rounded-lg border bg-card px-4 py-3 shadow-xs ..."

// AFTER
"... rounded-lg glass-surface border-transparent px-4 py-3 shadow-xs ..."
```

The full `m.li` className becomes:
```tsx
className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-lg glass-surface border-transparent px-4 py-3 shadow-xs transition-colors hover:bg-accent/5 focus-within:ring-1 focus-within:ring-ring/30 outline-none list-none"
```

**2. Add `Trash2` to the lucide-react import:**
```tsx
// BEFORE
import { Pencil, Check, X } from 'lucide-react'

// AFTER
import { Pencil, Check, X, Trash2 } from 'lucide-react'
```

**3. Replace the entire inline SVG in the delete button with:**
```tsx
<Trash2 size={14} aria-hidden="true" />
```

## Visual Notes

- `glass-surface` is defined in globals.css: `background: var(--glass-bg-strong); backdrop-filter: blur(calc(var(--glass-blur) / 2)); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);`
- In light mode: `var(--glass-bg-strong)` = `oklch(1 0 0 / 80%)` тАФ semi-transparent white. If this looks washed out against the violet gradient, fallback to `bg-white/5 border-white/10`
- In dark mode: `var(--glass-bg-strong)` = `oklch(1 0 0 / 14%)` тАФ subtle translucent layer
- `border-transparent` overrides the `border` from glass-surface to remove the visible border (keeping backdrop filter but removing the stroke)

## Technical Notes

- All existing Framer Motion animations (checkbox bounce, strikethrough scaleX, delete exit) are UNAFFECTED тАФ only className changes
- Tailwind v4 CSS-first: `glass-surface` is a custom @layer utility, already defined in globals.css
- Do NOT import anything new except `Trash2`

## UI Standard

- Pattern: list view (todo items)
- Standard: inline тАФ glass-surface items inside glassmorphism container for visual depth continuity
